behaviors.description: Clause extractor identifies all key contract terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties).
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: correctness is defined by semantic extraction completeness over real contract language ("identifies all key terms"). Hypothetical elsewhere: dispatch/merge wiring bugs are not part of this behavior. MOCK-MISS: this is not primarily a module-hand-off bug; it is extraction quality. REAL-INFRA: no explicit OS/network/subprocess requirement in behavior text. MOCK-HIDE: a stub extractor would always return expected terms and hide true misses. LLM-DEP: yes, output quality depends on real AI reviewer behavior and can vary by phrasing. MULTI-STEP: no, single reviewer behavior. Rejection gate: Workflow rejected because behavior text targets one reviewer, not a multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real clause-extractor model on a representative legal contract set with known key-term annotations; assert recall for parties/dates/obligations/penalties and fail on missed terms.

behaviors.description: Risk assessor flags policy-problematic language, including indirect/euphemistic phrasing.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: semantic judgment of indirect/euphemistic risky language against configured policy. Hypothetical elsewhere: pipeline concurrency/merge concerns are outside this behavior. MOCK-MISS: failure is in interpretation quality, not just wiring. REAL-INFRA: behavior does not require real OS/network effects to manifest. MOCK-HIDE: mocked outputs would mask inability to detect subtle phrasing. LLM-DEP: yes, depends on real model understanding and policy-sensitive classification quality. MULTI-STEP: no multi-agent chain required for this behavior alone. Rejection gate: Workflow rejected because only one reviewer’s decision quality is under test.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate the real risk-assessor model on a labeled set containing explicit and euphemistic risky clauses under the configured policy; assert true-positive coverage on indirect cases and controlled false positives.

behaviors.description: Compliance checker determines whether contract satisfies jurisdiction-specific regulations.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: correctness of regulatory compliance determination for configured jurisdiction by an AI reviewer. Hypothetical elsewhere: orchestrator and merge reliability are not required here. MOCK-MISS: this is decision-quality, not merely data passing. REAL-INFRA: no explicit need for real OS/network/subprocess in behavior text. MOCK-HIDE: deterministic stubs would hide misclassification of nuanced legal compliance. LLM-DEP: yes, correctness depends on real model output quality for legal interpretation. MULTI-STEP: no, single reviewer behavior. Rejection gate: System rejected because behavior does not specify infrastructure-side failure; Workflow rejected because no multi-step agent pipeline is required.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real compliance-checker model on jurisdiction-labeled contracts with known pass/fail outcomes; assert correct compliance verdicts and required-justification coverage for that jurisdiction.

behaviors.description: Merge step preserves conflicting assessments from different reviewers with attribution.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: deterministic merge rule for conflicting reviewer outputs (preserve both with attribution). Hypothetical elsewhere: reviewer model quality and pipeline dispatch are not part of this behavior. MOCK-MISS: no; failure is in merge logic itself and can be triggered with local fixtures. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess requirement. MOCK-HIDE: no, because direct structured inputs to merge function expose the bug. LLM-DEP: no, behavior does not depend on real AI output quality, only provided conflicting records. MULTI-STEP: no. Rejection gate: Integration rejected because behavior text scopes to merge-step conflict handling, which can be validated at one local function/object boundary without module I/O.
behaviors.test_description: Call the merge function/object with crafted extractor and risk-assessor records for the same clause with conflicting labels; assert output contains both assessments and correct reviewer attribution fields.

behaviors.description: End-to-end pipeline dispatches all three reviewers in parallel, collects outputs, merges report, and retains all findings on a real contract.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: full sequence across three AI reviewers plus merge, explicitly end-to-end and parallel, on a real contract, with no loss of findings. Hypothetical elsewhere: none needed; workflow behavior is explicit. MOCK-MISS: yes, isolated mocks can miss orchestration loss/race and cross-step omissions. REAL-INFRA: may involve runtime concurrency and service calls, but core minimum is dominated by multi-agent AI sequence correctness. LLM-DEP: yes, each reviewer is an AI model call. MULTI-STEP: yes, multiple real agentic steps in one pipeline. Integration brake: (a) real infra may matter, but (b) real AI output is required, and (c) multi-agent chain is explicit, so level must be Workflow. Rejection gate: Agentic rejected because behavior text requires the chained pipeline (dispatch→collect→merge) rather than a single model step.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real pipeline on real contract documents: run all three reviewers concurrently, await all results, merge, and assert every reviewer finding is present in final report with no dropped entries and correct cross-review attribution.
